title: Mexican Navy Captures Los Mayos Leader Omar Torres 'El Patas' in Culiacán — 11 Killed
detail: Mexican Navy forces raided properties in Culiacán linked to the La Mayiza faction and captured Omar Oswaldo Torres Cabada, known as 'El Patas', a senior leader within the Los Mayos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel. Torres faces a US arrest warrant out of San Diego, California. Navy forces were attacked upon arrival and returned fire, killing 11 alleged gunmen. Authorities seized high-powered weapons and tactical equipment at the scene. The operation was part of broader security crackdowns under President Claudia Sheinbaum amid ongoing US pressure to deliver results against organized crime.

media.caption: Mexican Navy captures Los Mayos leader Omar Torres El Patas in Culiacan, 11 killed

title: El Mayo's Daughter Mónica Zambada Briefly Detained in Culiacán, Then Released
detail: During the March 19 Navy operations in the El Salado district of Culiacán, Mexican authorities briefly detained Mónica Zambada Niebla, daughter of imprisoned Sinaloa Cartel co-founder Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada, along with a minor. Security Secretary Omar García Harfuch stated she was held to verify outstanding warrants and ensure safety, not arrested. Authorities found no active warrants against her in Mexico or the US and released her. She subsequently obtained a court injunction (amparo) from Judge Mariana Aramburo Rojo prohibiting future detention or deportation without a warrant. Controversy persists as Mónica remains on the US Treasury OFAC sanctions list for alleged money laundering on behalf of her father.

media.caption: El Mayo's daughter Monica Zambada briefly detained then released in Culiacan
